fields.title: ADMountStyle
fields.description: The network home protocol to use: `afp` or `smb`.

fields.title: ADCreateMobileAccountAtLoginFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADCreateMobileAccountAtLogin` key.

fields.title: ADCreateMobileAccountAtLogin
fields.description: If `true`, the system creates a mobile account at login.

fields.title: ADWarnUserBeforeCreatingMAFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADWarnUserBeforeCreatingMA` key.

fields.title: ADWarnUserBeforeCreatingMA
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the warning before creating the mobile account.

fields.title: ADForceHomeLocalFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADForceHomeLocal` key.

fields.title: ADForceHomeLocal
fields.description: If `true`, the system forces a local home directory.

fields.title: ADUseWindowsUNCPathFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADUseWindowsUNCPath` key.

fields.title: ADUseWindowsUNCPath
fields.description: If `true`, the system uses the UNC path from Active Directory to derive the network home location.

fields.title: ADAllowMultiDomainAuthFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADAllowMultiDomainAuth` key.

fields.title: ADAllowMultiDomainAuth
fields.description: If `true`, the system allows authentication from any domain in the namespace.

fields.title: ADDefaultUserShellFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADDefaultUserShell` key.

fields.title: ADDefaultUserShell
fields.description: The default user shell.

fields.title: ADMapUIDAttributeFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADMapUIDAttribute` key.

fields.title: ADMapUIDAttribute
fields.description: The map UID to attribute.

fields.title: ADMapGIDAttributeFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADMapGIDAttribute` key.

fields.title: ADMapGIDAttribute
fields.description: The map GID to attribute.

fields.title: ADMapGGIDAttributeFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADMapGGIDAttributeFlag` key.

fields.title: ADMapGGIDAttribute
fields.description: The map group GID to attribute.

fields.title: ADPreferredDCServerFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADPreferredDCServer` key.

fields.title: ADPreferredDCServer
fields.description: The preferred domain server.

fields.title: ADDomainAdminGroupListFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADDomainAdminGroupList` key.

fields.title: ADDomainAdminGroupList
fields.description: The list of Active Directory groups with admin access.

fields.title: ADNamespaceFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADNamespace` key.

fields.title: ADNamespace
fields.description: The primary user account naming convention; either `forest` or `domain`.

fields.title: ADPacketSignFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADPacketSign` key.

fields.title: ADPacketSign
fields.description: The packet signing policy.

fields.title: ADPacketEncryptFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADPacketEncrypt` key.

fields.title: ADPacketEncrypt
fields.description: The packet encryption policy.

fields.title: ADRestrictDDNSFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADRestrictDDNS` key.

fields.title: ADRestrictDDNS
fields.description: An array of strings that represent the interfaces allowed for dynamic DNS updates, such as en0 and en1.

fields.title: ADTrustChangePassIntervalDaysFlag
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the `ADTrustChangePassIntervalDays` key.

fields.title: ADTrustChangePassIntervalDays
fields.description: The number of days before requiring a change of the computer trust account password. Set to `0` to disable the feature.

entries.title: DNS Proxy
entries.description: The payload that configures DNS proxies.

fields.title: App Bundle Identifier
fields.description: The bundle identifier of the app containing the DNS proxy network extension.

fields.title: Provider Bundle Identifier
fields.description: The bundle identifier of the DNS proxy network extension to use. Declaring the bundle identifier is useful for apps that contain more than one DNS proxy extension.

fields.title: Provider Configuration
fields.description: The dictionary of vendor-specific configuration items.

fields.title: DNS Proxy UUID
fields.description: A globally unique identifier for this DNS proxy configuration. The proxy processes DNS lookups traffic for managed apps with the same `DNSProxyUUID` in their app attributes. This key is required for user enrollment.

entries.title: DNS Settings
entries.description: The payload that configures encrypted DNS settings.

fields.title: DNS Settings
fields.description: A dictionary that defines a configuration for an encrypted DNS server.

fields.title: On Demand Rules
fields.description: An array of rules that define the DNS settings. If not set, the system always applies the DNS settings. These rules are identical to the `OnDemandRules` array in VPN payloads.

fields.title: Prohibit Disablement
fields.description: If `true`, the system prohibits users from disabling DNS settings. This key is only available on supervised devices.

entries.title: Dock
entries.description: The payload that configures the Dock.

fields.title: Tilesize
fields.description: The tile size. Values must be in the range from 16 to 128.

fields.title: Size Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, locks the size slider.

fields.title: Magnification
fields.description: If `true`, enables magnification.

fields.title: Magnify Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, locks magnification.

fields.title: Largesize
fields.description: The size of the largest magnification.

fields.title: Magsize Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, locks the magnification slider.

fields.title: Orientation
fields.description: The orientation of the Dock.

fields.title: Position Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, locks the position.

fields.title: Mineffect
fields.description: The minimize effect.

fields.title: Mineffect Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, locks "Minimize windows using."

fields.title: Windowtabbing
fields.description: Set the "Prefer tabs when opening documents" to the provided value.

fields.title: Windowtabbing Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, disables "Prefer tabs when opening documents" checkbox.

fields.title: Dblclickbehavior
fields.description: The behavior when the window's title bar is double-clicked.

fields.title: Dblclickbehavior Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, locks "Double-click a window's title bar."

fields.title: Minimize To Application
fields.description: If `true`, enables "Minimize windows into application icon."

fields.title: Minintoapp Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, disables the "Minimize windows into application icon" checkbox.

fields.title: Launchanim
fields.description: If `true`, enables "Animate opening applications."

fields.title: Launchanim Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, locks "Animate opening applications."

fields.title: Autohide
fields.description: If `true`, enables "Automatically hide and show the Dock."

fields.title: Autohide Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, locks "Automatically hide."

fields.title: Show Process Indicators
fields.description: If true, shows the process indicator.

fields.title: Showindicators Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, locks "Show indicators."

fields.title: Show Recents
fields.description: If `true`, enables "Show recent items."

fields.title: Showrecents Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, disables "Show recent applications" checkbox.

fields.title: Contents Immutable
fields.description: If `true`, disables changes to the Dock.

fields.title: MCXDockSpecialFolders
fields.description:
One or more special folders that may be created at user login time and placed in the Dock.



The "My Applications" item is only used for Simple Finder environments. The "Original Network Home" item is only used for mobile account users.

fields.title: AllowDockFixupOverride
fields.description: If `true`, use the file in `/Library/Preferences/com.apple.dockfixup.plist` when a new user or migrated user logs in. This option has no effect for existing users. Available in macOS 10.12 and later. Only available on the device channel.

fields.title: Static Only
fields.description: If `true`, uses the `static-apps` and `static-others` dictionaries for the Dock and ignores any items in the `persistent-apps` and `persistent-others` dictionaries. If `false`, the contents are merged with the static items listed first.

fields.title: Static Others
fields.description: An array of items located on the Documents side of the Dock and cannot be removed from that location.

fields.title: Static Apps
fields.description: An array of items located on the Applications side of the Dock and cannot be removed from that location.

fields.title: Persistent Apps
fields.description: An array of items located on the Applications side of the Dock that can be removed from the Dock.

fields.title: Persistent Others
fields.description: An array of items located on the Documents side of the Dock that can be removed from the Dock.

entries.title: Domains
entries.description: The payload that configures the domains under an organization's management.

fields.title: Email Domains
fields.description:
An array of domains. Mail marks in red all email addresses that lack a suffix matching any of these strings.

Available in iOS 8 and later and macOS 10.10 and later.

fields.title: Web Domains
fields.description:
An array of domains. The system considers URLs matching the patterns listed in this property managed.

Available in iOS 9.3 and later.

fields.title: Password Autofill Domains
fields.description:
An array of domains. Users can only save passwords in Safari from URLs matching the patterns listed here. This property doesn't disable the autofill feature itself.

Supervised devices or Shared iPads need this property to enable saving passwords in Safari.

Available in iOS 9.3 and later.

fields.title: Cross-Site Tracking Prevention Relaxed Domains
fields.description:
An array of up to 10 strings. URLs matching the patterns listed here have relaxed enforcement of cross-site tracking prevention.

Available in iOS 16.2 and later and macOS 13.1 and later.

fields.title: Cross-Site Tracking Prevention Relaxed Apps
fields.description:
An array of up to 10 strings representing app bundle-ids. Apps matching the bundle-ids listed here have relaxed enforcement of cross-site tracking prevention for the domains listed in `CrossSiteTrackingPreventionRelaxedDomains`.

Available in iOS 18 and later and macOS 15 and later.

entries.title: Education Configuration
entries.description: The payload that configures the users, groups, and departments within an educational organization.

fields.title: OrganizationUUID
fields.description: The organization's UUID identifier. This identifier can be any valid UUID. All teacher and student devices that need to communicate with one another must have the same organization UUID, particularly if they originated from different Device Enrollment Programs.

fields.title: OrganizationName
fields.description: The organization's display name. The system displays this name in the iOS login screen.

fields.title: PayloadCertificateUUID
fields.description:
The UUID of an identity certificate payload within the same profile to use for performing client authentication with other devices. This property supports PKCS12 certificates.

Required to configure Classroom. Has no effect on the configuration of the Shared iPad login screen.

fields.title: LeaderPayloadCertificateAnchorUUID
fields.description:
The array of UUIDs referring to certificate payloads within the same profile that the system uses to authorize leader peer certificate identities. This array needs to contain all necessary certificates to validate the entire chain of trust. Leader certificates needs to have the common name prefix leader, which is case insensitive.

This property doesn't support identity payloads or PKCS12 certificates.

Required when configuring a student device for Classroom, and ignored when configuring an instructor device. Has no effect on the configuration of the Shared iPad login screen.

fields.title: MemberPayloadCertificateAnchorUUID
fields.description:
The array of UUIDs referring to certificate payloads within the same profile that the system uses to authorize group member peer certificate identities. This array must contain all certificates needed to validate the entire chain of trust. Member certificates must have the common name prefix member (case insensitive).

This property doesn't support identity payloads or PKCS12 certificates.

Required when configuring a student device for Classroom, and ignored when configuring an instructor device. Has no effect on the configuration of the Shared iPad login screen.

fields.title: ResourcePayloadCertificateUUID
fields.description:
The UUID of an identity certificate payload within the same profile that the system uses to perform client authentication when fetching additional resources, such as student images.

If set, the system uses this key to configure both Classroom and the Shared iPad login screen. If not set, the system uses MDM client identity.

fields.title: UserIdentifier
fields.description:
The unique string that identifies the user of this device within the organization.

Don't set this value in payloads intended to configure the Shared iPad login screen.

fields.title: Departments
fields.description: _For Shared iPad profiles:_ The array of dictionaries that defines which departments the system displays in the Shared iPad login screen. If set, the system uses this key to configure both Classroom and the Shared iPad login screen.

fields.title: Groups
fields.description:
_For Shared iPad profiles:_ The array of dictionaries that defines which groups the user can select in the Login Window.

_For leader/teacher profiles:_ The array of dictionaries that defines the groups that the user can control.

_For member/student profiles:_ The array of dictionaries that defines the groups where the user is a member.

fields.title: Users
fields.description:
For Shared iPad profiles: The array of dictionaries that define the users that the system displays in the iOS Login Window.

_For leader/teacher profiles:_ The array of dictionaries that define users that are members of the teacher's groups.

_For member/student profiles:_ The array of dictionaries that needs to contain the definition of the user specified in the `UserIdentifier` key. With one-to-one member devices, this key should include only the device user and the teacher but not other class members.

fields.title: DeviceGroups
fields.description: _For leader/teacher profiles:_ The array of dictionaries that defines which device groups the leader can assign devices to. Not included in member payloads.

fields.title: ScreenObservationPermissionModificationAllowed
fields.description: If `true`, the system allows students enrolled in managed classes to modify their teacher's permissions for screen observation on their device.

entries.title: Energy Saver
entries.description: The payload that configures Energy Saver settings.

fields.title: EnergySaver Desktop ACPower
fields.description: The settings for a desktop computer.

fields.title: EnergySaver Portable ACPower
fields.description: The settings for a laptop computer using AC power.

fields.title: EnergySaver Portable BatteryPower
fields.description: The settings for a laptop computer using battery power.

fields.title: EnergySaver Desktop Schedule
fields.description: The schedule for turning a computer on and off.

fields.title: SleepDisabled
fields.description: If `true`, disables sleep.

entries.title: Exchange ActiveSync
entries.description: The payload that configures Exchange ActiveSync accounts.

fields.title: Email Address
fields.description: The full email address for the account. If not present in the payload, the device prompts for this string during profile installation.

fields.title: Exchange ActiveSync Host
fields.description: The Exchange server host name or IP address.

fields.title: Use SSL
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables SSL for authentication.

fields.title: Use OAuth
fields.description:
If `true`, enables OAuth for authentication. If enabled, don't specify a password.

Available only in iOS 12.0 and above.

fields.title: User
fields.description: This user name for this Exchange account. Required for noninteractive installations like MDM in iOS.

fields.title: Password
fields.description: The password of the account. Use only with encrypted profiles.

fields.title: Authentication Credential
fields.description: The `.p12` identity certificate in NSData blob format, for accounts that allow authentication via certificate.

fields.title: Authentication Credential Name
fields.description: The name or description of the certificate.

fields.title: Authentication Credential Password
fields.description: The password necessary for the `.p12` identity certificate. Used with mandatory encryption of profiles.

fields.title: Prevent Move
fields.description: If `true`, the system prevents moving messages from out of this email account into another account. This setting also prevents forwarding or replying from an account other than the recipient of the message.

fields.title: Prevent App Sheet
fields.description: If `true`, prevents this account from sending mail in any app other than the Apple Mail app.

fields.title: Payload Certificate UUID
fields.description: The UUID of the certificate payload within the same profile to use for the identity credential. If this field is present, the Certificate field isn't used.

fields.title: S/MIME Enabled
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption. In iOS 10.0 and later, this key is ignored. Use `SMIMESigningEnabled` instead.

fields.title: S/MIME Signing Enabled
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME signing for this account. Available in iOS 10.0 and later.

fields.title: S/MIME Signing Certificate
fields.description: The UUID of the identity certificate used to sign messages sent from this account.

fields.title: S/MIME Encryption Enabled
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption for this account. Available in iOS 10.0 and later. As of iOS 12.0, this key is deprecated. Use `SMIMEEncryptByDefault` instead.

fields.title: S/MIME Encryption Certificate
fields.description: The payload UUID of the identity certificate used to decrypt messages sent to this account. The system attaches the public certificate to outgoing mail to allow the user to receive encrypted mail. When the user sends encrypted mail, the system uses the public certificate to encrypt the copy of the mail in the user's Sent mailbox.

fields.title: S/MIME Enable Per-Message Switch
fields.description:
If `true`, the system displays the per-message encryption switch in the Mail Compose UI.

Available in iOS 8.0 and later. As of iOS 12.0, this key is deprecated. Use `SMIMEEnableEncryptionPerMessageSwitch` instead.

fields.title: Disable Mail Recents Syncing
fields.description: If `true`, the system excludes this account from Recent Addresses syncing.

fields.title: Past Days of Mail to Sync
fields.description:
The number of days in the past to sync mail on the device.

For no limit, use the value `0`.

fields.title: HeaderMagic
fields.description: The value of the `X-Apple-Config-Magic` header in each EAS HTTP request.

fields.title: Communication Service Rules
fields.description: The communication service handler rules for this account.

fields.title: Allow Mail Drop
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables this account to use Mail Drop.

fields.title: SMIMESigningUserOverrideable
fields.description: If `true`, the user can turn S/MIME signing on or off in Settings. Available in iOS 12.0 and later.

fields.title: SMIMESigningCertificateUUIDUserOverrideable
fields.description: If `true`, the user can select the signing identity. Available in iOS 12.0 and later.

fields.title: SMIMEEncryptByDefault
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption by default. If `SMIMEEnableEncryptionPerMessageSwitch` is `false`, the user can't change this default. Available in iOS 12.0 and later.

fields.title: SMIMEEncryptByDefaultUserOverrideable
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables encryption by default and the user can't change it. Available in iOS 12.0 and later.

fields.title: SMIMEEncryptionCertificateUUIDUserOverrideable
fields.description: If `true`, the user can select the S/MIME encryption identity, and encryption is on.Available in iOS 12.0 and later.

fields.title: SMIMEEnableEncryptionPerMessageSwitch
fields.description: If `true`, the system displays the per-message encryption switch in the Mail Compose UI. Available in iOS 12.0 and later.

fields.title: EnableMail
fields.description:
If `false`, the system disables the Mail service for this account. The user can reenable Mail service in Settings unless `EnableMailUserOverridable` is `false`.

> Note:
> At least of the following fields needs to be `true`: `EnableMail`, `EnableContacts`, `EnableCalendars`, `EnableReminders`, and `EnableNotes`.

fields.title: EnableContacts
fields.description:
If `false`, the system disables the Contacts service for this account. The user can reenable Contacts service in Settings unless `EnableContactsUserOverridable` is `false`.

> Note:
> At least of the following fields needs to be `true`: `EnableMail`, `EnableContacts`, `EnableCalendars`, `EnableReminders`, and `EnableNotes`.

fields.title: EnableCalendars
fields.description:
If `false`, the system disables the Calendars service for this account. The user can reenable Calendars service in Settings unless `EnableCalendarsUserOverridable` is `false`.

> Note:
> At least of the following fields needs to be `true`: `EnableMail`, `EnableContacts`, `EnableCalendars`, `EnableReminders`, and `EnableNotes`.

fields.title: EnableReminders
fields.description:
If `false`, the system disables the Reminders service for this account. The user can reenable Reminders service in Settings unless `EnableRemindersUserOverridable` is `false`.

> Note:
> At least of the following fields needs to be `true`: `EnableMail`, `EnableContacts`, `EnableCalendars`, `EnableReminders`, and `EnableNotes`.

fields.title: EnableNotes
fields.description:
If `false`, the system disables the Notes service for this account. The user can reenable Notes service in Settings unless `EnableNotesUserOverridable` is `false`.

> Note:
> At least of the following fields needs to be `true`: `EnableMail`, `EnableContacts`, `EnableCalendars`, `EnableReminders`, and `EnableNotes`.

fields.title: EnableMailUserOverridable
fields.description: If `false`, the system prevents the user from changing the state of the Mail service for this account in Settings.

fields.title: EnableContactsUserOverridable
fields.description: If `false`, the system prevents the user from changing the state of the Contacts service for this account in Settings.

fields.title: EnableCalendarsUserOverridable
fields.description: If `false`, the system prevents the user from changing the state of the Calendars service for this account in Settings.

fields.title: EnableRemindersUserOverridable
fields.description: If `false`, the system prevents the user from changing the state of the Reminders service for this account in Settings.

fields.title: EnableNotesUserOverridable
fields.description: If `false`, prevents the user from changing the state of the Notes service for this account in Settings.

fields.title: OAuthSignInURL
fields.description: The URL that this account should use for signing in through OAuth. Ignored unless `OAuth` is `true`. If you specify this URL, auto-discovery isn't used for this account, so you need to also specify a host.

fields.title: OAuthTokenRequestURL
fields.description: The URL that this account should use for token requests through OAuth. Ignored unless `OAuth` is `true`.

fields.title: OverridePreviousPassword
fields.description: If `true`, the system overrides the previous user/EAS password with the new EAS password in the payload. Available in iOS 14 and later.

fields.title: VPNUUID
fields.description: The VPNUUID of the per-app VPN the account uses for network communication. Available in iOS 14 and later.

entries.title: Exchange Web Services
entries.description: The payload that configures an Exchange Web Services accounts.

fields.title: EmailAddress
fields.description: The full email address for the account. If the email address string isn't present in the payload, the device prompts for it during profile installation.

fields.title: Host
fields.description: The Exchange server host name or IP address. Ignored if using OAuth.

fields.title: SSL
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables SSL.

fields.title: Use OAuth
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables OAuth for authentication. Don't specify a password if `OAuth` is `true`. Available in macOS 10.14 and later

fields.title: URL for OAuth sign-in
fields.description: The URL to load into a web view for authentication through OAuth when autodiscovery isn't used. This setting requires a `Host` value.

fields.title: UserName
fields.description: The user name for this Exchange account. Required for noninteractive installation, such as through MDM. If missing, the system prompts the user for it during interactive profile installation.

fields.title: Password
fields.description: The password of the account. Use only with encrypted profiles.

fields.title: Payload Certificate UUID
fields.description: The UUID of the certificate payload within the same profile to use for the identity credential. Supported on macOS 10.12 or later.

fields.title: AuthenticationCertificateUUID
fields.description: The UUID of the certificate payload within the same profile to use for the identity credential. Supported on macOS 10.11 or later. On macOS 10.12 or later use the PayloadCertificateUUID.

fields.title: Allow Mail Drop
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables Mail Drop.

fields.title: Path
fields.description: The server path.

fields.title: Port
fields.description: The server port number.

fields.title: ExternalHost
fields.description: The external server address.

fields.title: ExternalSSL
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables SSL for connections to the external server.

fields.title: ExternalPath
fields.description: The external server path.

fields.title: ExternalPort
fields.description: The external server port number.

entries.title: Extensible Single Sign-On
entries.description: The payload that configures an app extension that performs single sign-on (SSO).

notes: Requires user-approved MDM on at least one platform.

fields.title: ExtensionIdentifier
fields.description: The bundle identifier of the app extension that performs SSO for the specified URLs.

fields.title: TeamIdentifier
fields.description: The team identifier of the app extension. This key is required on macOS and ignored elsewhere.

fields.title: Type
fields.description: The type of SSO.

fields.title: Realm
fields.description: The realm name for `Credential` payloads. Use proper capitalization for this value. Ignored for `Redirect` payloads.

fields.title: ExtensionData
fields.description: A dictionary of arbitrary data passed through to the app extension.

fields.title: URLs
fields.description:
An array of URL prefixes of identity providers where the app extension performs SSO.

Required for `Redirect` payloads. Ignored for `Credential` payloads.

The URLs need to begin with `http://` or `https://`.

The system:

- Matches scheme and host name case-insensitively
- Doesn't allow query parameters and URL fragments
- Requires that the URLs of all installed Extensible SSO payloads are unique

fields.title: Hosts
fields.description:
An array of host or domain names that apps can authenticate through the app extension.

Required for `Credential` payloads. Ignored for `Redirect` payloads.

The system:

- Matches host or domain names case-insensitively
- Requires that all the host and domain names of all installed Extensible SSO payloads are unique

> Note:
> Host names that begin with a "." are wildcard suffixes that match all subdomains; otherwise the host name needs be an exact match.

fields.title: ScreenLockedBehavior
fields.description: If set to `Cancel`, the system cancels authentication requests when the screen is locked. If set to `DoNotHandle`, the request continues without SSO instead. This doesn't apply to requests where `userInterfaceEnabled` is `false`, or for background `URLSession` requests. Available in iOS 15 and later, and macOS 12 and later.

fields.title: DeniedBundleIdentifiers
fields.description: An array of bundle identifiers of apps that don't use SSO provided by this extension. Available in iOS 15 and later, and macOS 12 and later.

fields.title: AuthenticationMethod
fields.description: The Platform SSO authentication method the extension uses. Requires that the SSO Extension also supports the method. Available in macOS 13 and later, and deprecated in macOS 14.

fields.title: RegistrationToken
fields.description: The token this device uses for registration with Platform SSO. Use it for silent registration with the Identity Provider. Requires that `AuthenticationMethod` in `PlatformSSO` isn't empty. Available in macOS 13 and later.

fields.title: PlatformSSO
fields.description: The dictionary to configure Platform SSO. Requires `Type` to be set to `Redirect`.

entries.title: Extensible Single Sign-On (Kerberos)
entries.description: The payload that configures an app extension that performs single sign-on with the Kerberos extension.

notes: Requires user-approved MDM on at least one platform.

fields.title: ExtensionIdentifier
fields.description: Set this to `com.apple.AppSSOKerberos.KerberosExtension` for this extension.

fields.title: TeamIdentifier
fields.description: Set this to `apple` for this extension.

fields.title: Type
fields.description: Set this to `Credential` for this extension.

fields.title: Realm
fields.description: The Kerberos realm. Use proper capitalization for this value. If in an Active Directory forest, this is the realm where the user logs in.

fields.title: ExtensionData
fields.description: This is the dictionary used by the Apple built-in Kerberos extension.

fields.title: Hosts
fields.description:
One or more host or domain names for which the app extension performs SSO.

The system:

- Matches host or domain names case-insensitively
- Requires that all the host and domain names of all installed Extensible SSO payloads are unique

> Note:
> Host names that begin with a "." are wildcard suffixes that match all subdomains; otherwise the host name needs be an exact match.

entries.title: FDE FileVault
entries.description: The payload that configures FileVault.

notes: Requires user-approved MDM on at least one platform.

fields.title: Enable
fields.description: Set to `On` to enable FileVault and set to `Off` to disable FileVault. Payloads set to `On` sent through MDM need to either include full authentication information in the payload or have the `Defer` option set to `true`. When `Defer` is `true`, the system prompts for the authentication information when the user enables FileVault.

fields.title: Defer
fields.description: If `true`, the system defers enabling FileVault until the designated user logs out. For details, see `fdesetup(8)`. Only a local user or a mobile account user can enable FileVault.

fields.title: UserEntersMissingInfo
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables a prompt for missing user name or password fields.

fields.title: UseRecoveryKey
fields.description: If `true`, the system creates a personal recovery key and displays it to the user.

fields.title: ShowRecoveryKey
fields.description: If `false`, the system prevents display of the personal recovery key to the user after the system enables FileVault.

fields.title: OutputPath
fields.description: The path to the location of the recovery key and computer information property list.

fields.title: Certificate
fields.description: The DER-encoded certificate data if the system creates an institutional recovery key. This key isn't supported on a Mac with Apple silicon.

fields.title: PayloadCertificateUUID
fields.description: The UUID of the payload within the same profile containing the asymmetric recovery key certificate payload.

fields.title: Username
fields.description: The user name of the Open Directory user to add to FileVault.

fields.title: Password
fields.description: The password of the Open Directory user to add to FileVault. Use the `UserEntersMissingInfo` key to prompt for this information.

fields.title: UseKeychain
fields.description: If `true` and you don't include certificate information in this payload, the system uses the keychain created at `/Library/Keychains/FileVaultMaster.keychain` when it adds the institutional recovery key.

fields.title: DeferForceAtUserLoginMaxBypassAttempts
fields.description: The maximum number of times users can bypass enabling FileVault before the system requires the user to enable it to log in. If the value is `0`, the system requires the user to enable FileVault the next time they attempt to log in. Set this key to `-1` to disable this feature.

fields.title: DeferDontAskAtUserLogout
fields.description: If `true`, the system prevents requests to enable FileVault at user logout time.

fields.title: ForceEnableInSetupAssistant
fields.description:
If `true`, and installation of this payload occurs after enrolling with MDM in Setup Assistant, the system requests Setup Assistant to enable FileVault at setup time.

To use this, enable the Await Device Configured DEP configuration option and send this profile with this key set, before sending the `DeviceConfiguredCommand`.

An admin SecureToken user is required, otherwise the FileVault pane does not appear.

entries.title: FDE FileVault Options
entries.description: The payload that configures FileVault options.

fields.title: DontAllowFDEDisable
fields.description: If `true`, the system won't disable FileVault.

fields.title: DontAllowFDEEnable
fields.description: If `true`, the system won't enable FileVault.

fields.title: DestroyFVKeyOnStandby
fields.description: If `true`, the system won't store th FileVault key across restarts.

entries.title: FDE Recovery Key Escrow
entries.description: The payload that configures FileVault recovery key escrow.

fields.title: Location
fields.description: The description of the location where the system escrows the recovery key. The system inserts this text into the message the user sees when it enables FileVault.

fields.title: EncryptCertPayloadUUID
fields.description: The UUID of a payload within the same profile that contains the certificate that the system uses to encrypt the recovery key. The referenced payload must be of type `com.apple.security.pkcs1`.

fields.title: DeviceKey
fields.description:
The string that's included in help text if the user appears to have forgotten the password. Site admins can use this key to look up the escrowed key for the particular computer.

This key replaces the `RecordNumber` key used in the previous escrow mechanism. If the key is missing, the system uses the device serial number instead.

entries.title: FDE Recovery Key Redirection
entries.description: The payload that configures FileVault recovery key redirection.

fields.title: RedirectURL
fields.description: The URL to which FDE recovery keys should be sent instead of to Apple. The URL must begin with https://.

fields.title: EncryptCertPayloadUUID
fields.description: The UUID of a payload within the same profile that contains a certificate used to encrypt the recovery key when it's sent to the redirected URL. The referenced payload must be of type \`com.apple.security.pkcs1\`.

entries.title: File Provider
entries.description: The payload that configures file provider settings.

notes: Requires user-approved MDM on at least one platform.

fields.title: ManagementAllowsRemoteSyncing
fields.description: If `false`, the device prevents the File Provider extension from using synchronization in any app. Also, none of the other options will be evaluated. Synchronization will be totally disabled for any application.

fields.title: ManagementRemoteSyncingAllowList
fields.description: An array of strings representing the composed identifiers of apps. The device allows the corresponding apps to use File Provider extension synchronization. If present, and `ManagementAllowsRemoteSyncing` is set to `true`, the device allows only the apps in this list to use synchronization. This key is ignored if `ManagementAllowsRemoteSyncing` is set to `false`. If present, the other options will only be evaluated for the apps in this list. The format of the app identifiers is "Bundle-ID (Team-ID)", for example `com.example.app (ABCD1234)`.

fields.title: AllowManagedFileProvidersToRequestAttribution
fields.description: If `true`, enables file providers access to the path of the requesting process.

fields.title: ManagementAllowsKnownFolderSyncing
fields.description: If `false`, the device prevents the File Provider extension from using desktop and documents synchronization in any app. This does not impact the ability for apps to utilize the File Provider extension for file and folder syncing with remote storage.

fields.title: ManagementKnownFolderSyncingAllowList
fields.description: An array of strings representing the composed identifiers of apps. The device allows the corresponding apps to use File Provider extension desktop and documents synchronization. If present, and `ManagementAllowsKnownFolderSyncing` is set to `true`, the device allows only the apps in this list to use desktop and documents synchronization. This key is ignored if `ManagementAllowsKnownFolderSyncing` is set to `false`. This setting does not impact the ability for apps to use File Provider extension volume access. The format of the app identifiers is "Bundle-ID (Team-ID)", for example `com.example.app (ABCD1234)`.

fields.title: ManagementAllowsExternalVolumeSyncing
fields.description: If `false`, the device only allows File Provider extension volume synchronization for the system "home" volume and any data separated volume, and prevents synchronization with any other volumes. If `true``, the device allows File Provider extension volume synchronization for the system "home" volume, any data separated volume, and any encrypted APFS volumes (on either internal or external media).

fields.title: ManagementExternalVolumeSyncingAllowList
fields.description: An array of strings representing the composed identifiers of apps. The device allows the corresponding apps to use File Provider extension volume synchronization. If present, and `ManagementAllowsExternalVolumeSyncing` is set to `true`, the device allows only the apps in this list to use volume synchronization. This key is ignored if `ManagementAllowsExternalVolumeSyncing` is set to `false`. The format of the app identifiers is "Bundle-ID (Team-ID)", for example `com.example.app (ABCD1234)`.

fields.title: ManagementDomainAutoEnablementList
fields.description: An array of strings representing the composed identifiers of apps. The device automatically enables the File Provider domains for the corresponding apps. The device doesn't enable existing domains if enrollment happens after they are created. The device doesn't prevent the user from disabling these File Provider domains. Users need to manually enable File Provider domains in the Finder if their corresponding apps aren't listed here. The format of the app identifiers is "Bundle-ID (Team-ID)", for example `com.example.app (ABCD1234)`.

entries.title: Finder
entries.description: The payload that configures Finder settings.

fields.title: ProhibitBurn
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables the Finder's burn support.

fields.title: InterfaceLevel
fields.description: Specifies whether Finder should operate in Simple or Full mode.

fields.title: ProhibitConnectTo
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables Connect to Server.

fields.title: ProhibitEject
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables Eject.

fields.title: ProhibitGoToFolder
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables Go to Folder.

fields.title: ShowExternalHardDrivesOnDesktop
fields.description: If `false`, the system doesn't show external hard drives on the Desktop.

fields.title: ShowHardDrivesOnDesktop
fields.description: If `false`, the system doesn't show internal hard drives on the Desktop.

fields.title: ShowMountedServersOnDesktop
fields.description: If `false`, the system doesn't show mounted file servers on the Desktop.

fields.title: ShowRemovableMediaOnDesktop
fields.description: If `false`, the system doesn't show removable media items on the Desktop.

fields.title: WarnOnEmptyTrash
fields.description: If `false`, the system doesn't warn the user before emptying the trash.

entries.title: Firewall
entries.description: The payload that configures the firewall.

fields.title: EnableFirewall
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables the firewall.

fields.title: BlockAllIncoming
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables blocking all incoming connections.

fields.title: EnableStealthMode
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables stealth mode.

fields.title: Applications
fields.description: The list of apps with connections that the firewall controls.

fields.title: EnableLogging
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables logging. Available in macOS 12 through macOS 14.6.

fields.title: LoggingOption
fields.description: The type of logging. Available in macOS 12 and through macOS 14.6.

fields.title: AllowSigned
fields.description:
If `true`, the system allows built-in software to receive incoming connections. Available in macOS 12.3 and later.

> Note:
> The system ensures that `AllowSigned` always has a value. If missing from the payload, the system sets it to `true`.

fields.title: AllowSignedApp
fields.description:
If `true`, the system allows downloaded signed software to receive incoming connections. Available in macOS 12.3 and later.

> Note:
> The system ensures that `AllowSignedApp` always has a value. If missing from the payload, the system sets it to `true`.

entries.title: Font
entries.description: The payload that configures fonts.

fields.title: Font Name
fields.description:
The user-visible name for the font. This field is replaced by the actual name of the font after installation. Each payload must contain exactly one font file in trueType (.ttf) or OpenType (.otf) format. Collection formats (.ttc or .otc) are not supported.

Fonts are identified by their embedded PostScript names. Two fonts with the same PostScript name are considered to be the same font even if their contents differ. Installing two different fonts with the same PostScript name isn't supported, and the resulting behavior is undefined.

fields.title: Font
fields.description: The contents of the font file.

entries.title: Global HTTP Proxy
entries.description: The payload that configures a global HTTP proxy.

notes: Requires supervised devices on at least one platform.

fields.title: Proxy Type
fields.description: The proxy type. For a manual proxy type, the profile contains the proxy server address, including its port, and optionally a user name and password. For an auto proxy type, you can enter a PAC URL.

fields.title: Proxy Server
fields.description: The proxy server's network address. The device requires this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores it if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`.

fields.title: Proxy Server Port
fields.description: The proxy server's port number. The device requires this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores this if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`.

fields.title: Proxy Username
fields.description: The user name used to authenticate to the proxy server. The device only uses this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores it if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`.

fields.title: Proxy Password
fields.description: The password used to authenticate to the proxy server. The device only uses this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores it if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`.

fields.title: Proxy PAC URL
fields.description: The URL of the PAC file that defines the proxy configuration. Starting in iOS 13 and macOS 10.15, only URLs that begin with `http://` or `https://` are allowed. This is only used if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`, and is ignored if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`.

fields.title: Proxy PAC Fallback Allowed
fields.description: If `true`, allows connecting directly to the destination if the proxy autoconfiguration (PAC) file is unreachable.

fields.title: Proxy Bypass Allowed
fields.description: If `true`, allows the device to bypass the proxy server to display the login page for captive networks.

entries.title: Global Preferences
entries.description: The payload to configure global preferences.

fields.title: MultipleSessionEnabled
fields.description: If `false`, disables fast user switching.

fields.title: Autologout AutoLogOutDelay
fields.description: The `autologout` delay, in seconds. A value of `0` means `autologout` is off. In some cases, this delay may be restricted to values between 5 minutes and 24 hours.

entries.title: Google Account
entries.description: The payload that configures a Google account.

fields.title: Account Description
fields.description: A user-visible description of the Google account, shown in the Mail and Settings apps.

fields.title: Account Name
fields.description: The user's full name for the Google account. This name appears in sent messages.

fields.title: Email Address
fields.description: The full Google email address for the account.

fields.title: Communication Service Rules
fields.description: The communication service handler rules for this account.

fields.title: VPNUUID
fields.description: The VPNUUID of the per-app VPN the account uses for network communication. Available in iOS 14 and later.

entries.title: Home Screen Layout
entries.description: The payload that configures the Home Screen layout.

notes: Requires supervised devices on at least one platform.

fields.title: Dock
fields.description: An array of dictionaries, each of which must conform to the icon dictionary format. If this key isn't present, the user's Dock is empty.

fields.title: Pages
fields.description: An array of arrays of dictionaries, each of which must conform to the icon dictionary format.

entries.title: Identification
entries.description: The payload that configures the names of the account user.

fields.title: PayloadIdentification
fields.description: The dictionary that contains details about the user.

entries.title: Identity Preference
entries.description: The payload that configures the user's identity on the device.

fields.title: Name
fields.description: The email address (in RFC 822 format), DNS host name, or other name that uniquely identifies a service requiring this identity.

fields.title: PayloadCertificateUUID
fields.description: The UUID of the certificate payload within the same profile to use for the identity credential.

entries.title: Jabber Account
entries.description: The payload that configures a Jabber account.

fields.title: Account Description
fields.description: The description of the account.

fields.title: Account Hostname
fields.description: The server's address.

fields.title: Account Username
fields.description: The user's user name.

fields.title: Account Password
fields.description: The user's password.

fields.title: Use SSL
fields.description: If `true`, enables SSL.

fields.title: Port Number
fields.description: The server's port.

fields.title: Jabber Authentication
fields.description: The authentication method for the account.

entries.title: LDAP
entries.description: The payload that configures a Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) account.

fields.title: Account Description
fields.description: The description of the account.

fields.title: Account Hostname
fields.description: The server's address.

fields.title: Account Username
fields.description: The user's user name.

fields.title: Account Password
fields.description: The user's password. Only use this in encrypted profiles.

fields.title: Use SSL
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables SSL.

fields.title: Search Settings
fields.description: An array of search settings dictionaries.

fields.title: VPNUUID
fields.description: The VPNUUID of the per-app VPN the account uses for network communication. Available in iOS 14 and later.

entries.title: Lights Out Management (LOM)
entries.description: The payload that configures lights-out management (LOM) settings.

notes: Requires user-approved MDM on at least one platform.

fields.title: Device Certificate payload UUID
fields.description: The UUID certificate for the device. This key indicates the device can receive `PowerON`, `PowerOFF`, and `Reset` requests from a LOM controller. This certificate must contain the Key Usage attributes of Digital Signature, Key Encipherment and Data Encipherment. As well as the Extended Key Usage attributes of Server Authentication and Client Authentication.

fields.title: Controller Certificate payload UUID
fields.description: The UUID certificate for the LOM controller. This key configures the device to accept the `LOMDeviceRequestCommand` from MDM and then send it to the target device.

fields.title: CA certificate payload UUIDs
fields.description: An array of payload UUIDs containing CA certificates that controllers use to evaluate trust of device certificates.

fields.title: CA certificate payload UUIDs
fields.description:
An array of payload UUIDs containing CA certificates that devices use to evaluate trust of controller certificates.

This key configures the device to accept the `LOMDeviceRequestCommand` from MDM and then send it to the target device. This certificate must contain the Key Usage attributes of Digital Signature, Key Encipherment and Data Encipherment. As well as the Extended Key Usage attributes of Server Authentication and Client Authentication.

entries.title: Lock Screen Message
entries.description: The payload that configures a Lock Screen message.

notes: Requires supervised devices on at least one platform.

fields.title: Asset Tag
fields.description: The asset tag information for the device, displayed in the Login Window and Lock Screen.

fields.title: If Lost message
fields.description: Deprecated. Use `LockScreenFootnote` instead.

fields.title: LockScreenFootnote
fields.description: The footnote displayed in the Login Window and Lock Screen.

entries.title: Login Items: Managed Items
entries.description: The payload that configures a device's login items.

fields.title: AutoLaunchedApplicationDictionary Managed
fields.description: An array of login item dictionaries.

entries.title: Login Window
entries.description: The payload that configures Login Window behavior.

fields.title: SHOWFULLNAME
fields.description: If `true`, the system shows the name and password dialog. If `false`, the system displays a list of users.

fields.title: HideLocalUsers
fields.description: If `true`, the system shows only network and system users when showing a user list.

fields.title: IncludeNetworkUser
fields.description: If `true`, the system shows network users when showing a user list.

fields.title: HideAdminUsers
fields.description: If `true`, the system hides administrator users when showing a user list.

fields.title: SHOWOTHERUSERS MANAGED
fields.description: If `true`, the system displays "Other..." when it shows a list of users.

fields.title: AdminHostInfo
fields.description: The admin host info. If present in the payload, the system displays its value in the Login Window as additional computer information. Before macOS 10.10, this string could only contain host name, system version, or IP address. After macOS 10.10, setting this key to any value allows the user to click the time area of the menu bar to toggle through various computer information values.

fields.title: AllowList
fields.description: The list of user GUIDs or group GUIDs of users that the system allows to log in. An asterisk (`*`) string specifies all users or groups. This only applies to network accounts and mobile accounts.

fields.title: DenyList
fields.description: The list of user GUIDs or group GUIDs of users that the system disallows to log in. This list takes priority over the list in the `AllowList` key. This only applies to network accounts and mobile accounts.

fields.title: HideMobileAccounts
fields.description: If `true`, the system hides mobile account users in a user list. In some cases, mobile users show up as network users.

fields.title: ShutDownDisabled
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables the Shut Down button.

fields.title: RestartDisabled
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables the Restart item.

fields.title: SleepDisabled
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables the Sleep button.

fields.title: DisableConsoleAccess
fields.description: If `true`, the system disregards the `>console` special user name, which provides a command line UI.

fields.title: LoginwindowText
fields.description: The text to display in the Login Window.

fields.title: ShutDownDisabledWhileLoggedIn
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables the Shut Down menu item when the user is logged in.

fields.title: RestartDisabledWhileLoggedIn
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables the Restart menu item when the user is logged in.

fields.title: PowerOffDisabledWhileLoggedIn
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables the Power Off menu item when the user is logged in.

fields.title: LogOutDisabledWhileLoggedIn
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables the Log Out menu item when the user is logged in. Available in macOS 10.13 and later.

fields.title: DisableScreenLockImmediate
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables the immediate Screen Lock functions. Available in macOS 10.13 and later.

fields.title: ShowInputMenu
fields.description: If `true`, the system shows the Input Menu in the Login Window.

fields.title: DisableFDEAutoLogin
fields.description: If `true`, the system disables the automatic login option when using FileVault.

fields.title: AutologinUsername
fields.description: The user short name for an existing user to set up auto login.

fields.title: AutologinPassword
fields.description: An optional user password to set up auto login. This must match the `AutologinUsername` user's current password.

entries.title: Login Window: Login Items
entries.description: The payload that configures login behavior.

fields.title: DisableLoginItemsSuppression
fields.description: If `true`, the system prevents the user from disabling login item launches by using the Shift key.

entries.title: Login Window: Scripts
entries.description: The payload that configures scripts to run at login and logout.

fields.title: Loginscripts
fields.description: An array of one or more dictionaries of scripts to run at user login time.

fields.title: Logoutscripts
fields.description: An array of one or more dictionaries of scripts to run at user logout time.

fields.title: SkipLoginHook
fields.description: If `true`, the system doesn't execute the login scripts during login.

fields.title: SkipLogoutHook
fields.description: If `true`, the system doesn't execute the logout scripts during logout.

entries.title: macOS Server Account
entries.description: The payload that configures a macOS Server account.

fields.title: Account Hostname
fields.description: The server's address.

fields.title: Account Username
fields.description: The user's user name.

fields.title: Account Password
fields.description: The user's password.

fields.title: Account Description
fields.description: The description of the account.

fields.title: Configured Accounts
fields.description: An array of dictionaries containing configured account types and relevant settings

entries.title: Mail
entries.description: The payload that configures a Mail account.

fields.title: Account Description
fields.description: A user-visible description of the email account, shown in the Mail and Settings applications.

fields.title: Account Name
fields.description: The full user name for the account. The system displays this name in sent messages.

fields.title: Account Type
fields.description: Defines the protocol to use for the account.

fields.title: Email Address
fields.description: The full email address for the account. If this string isn't present in the payload, the device prompts the user for this string during interactive profile installation in Settings or System Preferences.

fields.title: Incoming Mail Server Authentication
fields.description: The authentication scheme for incoming mail.

fields.title: Mail Server
fields.description: The incoming mail server host name.

fields.title: Port
fields.description: The incoming mail server port number. If not set, the system uses the default port for a given protocol.

fields.title: Use SSL
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables SSL for authentication on the incoming mail server.

fields.title: Username
fields.description: The user name for the email account, usually the same as the email address up to the "@" character. If not set and the account requires authentication for incoming email, the device prompts the user for this string during interactive profile installation in Settings or System Preferences.

fields.title: Password
fields.description: The password for the incoming mail server. Only use this in encrypted profiles.

fields.title: Password
fields.description: The password for the outgoing mail server. Only use this in encrypted profiles.

fields.title: Outgoing Password Same As Incoming
fields.description:
If `true`, the system prompts the user only once for the password, which it uses for both outgoing and incoming mail.

This setting is only supported by interactive profile installations. Not supported by non-interactive installations, such as MDM on iOS.

fields.title: Authentication Type
fields.description: The authentication scheme for outgoing mail.

fields.title: Mail Server
fields.description: The outgoing mail server host name.

fields.title: Port
fields.description: The outgoing mail server port number. If not set, the system uses ports 25, 587, and 465, in that order.

fields.title: Use SSL
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables SSL authentication on the outgoing mail server.

fields.title: Username
fields.description: The user name for the email account, usually the same as the email address up to the "@" character. If not set and the account requires authentication for outgoing email, the device prompts the user for this string during interactive profile installation in Settings or System Preferences.

fields.title: Prevent Move
fields.description: If `true`, the system prevents moving messages out of this email account and into another account. It also prevents forwarding or replying from an account other than the recipient of the message.

fields.title: Prevent App Sheet
fields.description: If `true`, the system prevents this account from sending mail in any app other than the Apple Mail app.

fields.title: S/MIME Enabled
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption. The system ignores this key in iOS 10.0 and later.

fields.title: S/MIME Signing Enabled
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME signing for this account.

fields.title: S/MIME Signing Certificate
fields.description: The payload UUID of the identity certificate used to sign messages sent from this account.

fields.title: S/MIME Encryption Enabled
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption for this account.

fields.title: S/MIME Encryption Certificate
fields.description: The UUID of the identity certificate used to decrypt messages sent to this account. The system attaches the public certificate to outgoing mail to allow the user to receive encrypted mail. When the user sends encrypted mail, the system uses the public certificate to encrypt the copy of the mail in their Sent mailbox.

fields.title: S/MIME Enable Per-Message Switch
fields.description: If `true`, the system displays the per-message encryption switch in the Mail Compose UI. Deprecated in iOS 12.0. Use `SMIMEEnableEncryptionPerMessageSwitch` instead.

fields.title: Disable Mail Recents Syncing
fields.description: If `true`, the system excludes this account from Recent Addresses syncing.

fields.title: Allow Mail Drop
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables this account to use Mail Drop.

fields.title: Path Prefix
fields.description: The path prefix for the IMAP mail server.

fields.title: SMIMESigningUserOverrideable
fields.description: If `true`, the user can turn S/MIME signing on or off in Settings.

fields.title: SMIMESigningCertificateUUIDUserOverrideable
fields.description: If `true`, the user can select the signing identity.

fields.title: SMIMEEncryptByDefault
fields.description: If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption by default.

fields.title: SMIMEEncryptByDefaultUserOverrideable
fields.description: If `true`, the user can turn encryption by default on/off, and encryption is on.

fields.title: SMIMEEncryptionCertificateUUIDUserOverrideable
fields.description: If `true`, the user can select the S/MIME encryption identity, and encryption is on.

fields.title: SMIMEEnableEncryptionPerMessageSwitch
fields.description: If `true`, the system displays the per-message encryption switch in the Mail Compose UI.

fields.title: VPNUUID
fields.description: The VPNUUID of the per-app VPN the account uses for network communication. Available in iOS 14 and later.

entries.title: Managed Preferences
entries.description: The payload that configures managed preferences.

fields.title: PayloadContent
fields.description: The dictionary containing app preference domains. The key names are application preference domain identifiers (for example, `com.example.my-app`), or the string `.GlobalPreferences` for the global domain. The values are the corresponding forced and set-once preferences.

entries.title: MDM
entries.description: The payload that configures mobile device management (MDM) settings.

notes: Requires supervised devices on at least one platform.

fields.title: Identity Certificate UUID
fields.description: The UUID of the certificate payload for the device's identity. It may also point to a SCEP payload.

fields.title: Topic
fields.description:
The topic that MDM listens to for push notifications. The certificate that the server uses to send push notifications must have the same topic in its subject. The topic must begin with the 'com.apple.mgmt.' prefix.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected.

fields.title: Server URL
fields.description:
The URL that the device contacts to retrieve device management instructions. The URL must begin with the `https://` URL scheme, and may contain a port number
(`:1234`, for example).

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected.

fields.title: Check In URL
fields.description:
The URL that the device should use to check in during installation. The URL must begin with the `https://` URL scheme and may contain a port number (`:1234`, for example). If not set, the system uses `ServerURL`.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected.

fields.title: Sign Message
fields.description: If 'true', each message coming from the device carries the additional 'Mdm-Signature' HTTP header.

fields.title: Access Rights
fields.description:
Logical OR of the following bit flags:

- `1`: Allow inspection of installed configuration profiles.
- `2`: Allow installation and removal of configuration profiles.
- `4`: Allow device lock and passcode removal.
- `8`: Allow device erase.
- `16`: Allow query of device information (device capacity, serial number).
- `32`: Allow query of network information (phone/SIM numbers, MAC addresses).
- `64`: Allow inspection of installed provisioning profiles.
- `128`: Allow installation and removal of provisioning profiles.
- `256`: Allow inspection of installed applications.
- `512`: Allow restriction-related queries.
- `1024`: Allow security-related queries.
- `2048`: Allow manipulation of settings.
- `4096`: Allow app management.

Don't set to `0`. Specify `1` if you specify `2`. Specify `64` if you specify `128`. Ignored if you set a value for `ManagedAppleID`.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the addition of any access right is an error, and the update is rejected.

fields.title: Use Development APNS
fields.description:
If 'true', the device uses the development APNS servers. Otherwise, the device uses the production servers.
Set to 'false' if your Apple Push Notification Service certificate was issued by the Apple Push Certificate Portal ('https://identity.apple.com/pushcert'). That portal only issues certificates for the production push environment.

fields.title: Managed Apple Account
fields.description:
The Managed Apple Account of the user. Previously required for profile-driven user enrollment.
Removed as of iOS 18 and macOS 15.

fields.title: Assigned Managed Apple Account
fields.description:
The Managed Apple Account pre-assigned to the authenticated user. Required for account-driven enrollments. Available in iOS 15 and later, and macOS 14 and later.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected.

fields.title: Enrollment Mode
fields.description:
The enrollment mode the server indicates to use when enrolling. Required for account-driven enrollment. Available in iOS 15 and macOS 14, and later.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected.

fields.title: ServerURLPinningCertificateUUIDs
fields.description: An array of strings, each containing the UUID of a certificate to use when evaluating trust to the '.../connect/' URLs of MDM servers.

fields.title: CheckInURLPinningCertificateUUIDs
fields.description: An array of strings, each containing the payload UUID of a certificate to use when evaluating trust to the '.../checkin/' URLs of MDM servers.

fields.title: PinningRevocationCheckRequired
fields.description:
If 'true', the system fails the connection attempt unless it obtains a verified positive response during certificate revocation checks.
If 'false', the system performs revocation checks on a best-attempt basis, where failure to reach the server isn't considered fatal.

fields.title: ServerCapabilities
fields.description:
A unique array of strings indicating server capabilities:

- `com.apple.mdm.per-user-connections`: Indicates that the server supports both device and user connections. This must be present when managing Shared iPad or macOS devices.
- `com.apple.mdm.bootstraptoken`: Indicates that the server supports escrowing the bootstrap token. This must be present for the device to create a bootstrap token and send it to the server. Available in iOS 26 and later, macOS 11 and later, and visionOS 26 and later.
- `com.apple.mdm.token`: Indicates that the server supports the `Get-Token` CheckIn message type. This must be present for the device to use `Get-Token` CheckIn message when appropriate.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the `com.apple.mdm.per-user-connections` capability must not be added or removed. Any such change is an error, and the update is rejected.

fields.title: CheckOutWhenRemoved
fields.description: If 'true', the device attempts to send a `Check-Out` message to the 'CheckInURL' when the profile is removed.

fields.title: RequiredAppIDForMDM
fields.description:
This property specifies an iTunes Store ID for an app the system can install with the InstallApplicationCommand, without any approval from the user. The MDM vendor or managing organization generally provides this app, which enhances the management experience for the user. The device shows the user details about this app in the account-driven enrollment process prior to installing the MDM profile. Use this property with account-driven MDM enrollments that normally require user approval for app installs through MDM.
Only account-driven enrollments support this property and other enrollment types ignore it.
Available in iOS 15.1 and later.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected.

fields.title: PromptUserToAllowBootstrapTokenForAuthentication
fields.description:
If 'true', the system warns the user that they need to reboot into RecoveryOS and allow the MDM to use the bootstrap token for authentication for certain sensitive operations such as enabling kernel extensions or installing some types of software updates. If the MDM doesn't need to perform these operations, it can leave this key set to 'false', and the user isn't notified.
The SettingsCommand.Command.Settings.MDMOptions.MDMOptions command overrides this default value.
This setting only applies to devices that have 'BootstrapTokenRequiredForSoftwareUpdate' or 'BootstrapTokenRequiredForKernelExtensionApproval' set to 'true' in their SecurityInfoResponse.SecurityInfo.
DEP-enrolled devices are automatically allowed to use the bootstrap token for authentication.
Available in macOS 11 and later.

entries.title: Media Management: Allowed Media
entries.description: The payload that configures media management.

fields.title: Logout Eject
fields.description: The media type dictionary that defines volumes to eject when the user logs out.

fields.title: Mount Controls
fields.description: The media type dictionary that controls volume mounting.

fields.title: Unmount Controls
fields.description: The media type dictionary that controls volume unmounting.

entries.title: Media Management: Disc Burning
entries.description: The payload that configures disc-burning settings.

fields.title: BurnSupport
fields.description:
Configure disc-burn. Allowed values:
- `off`: The system disables disc burning.
- `on`: The system allows normal default operation. Setting this key to `on` doesn't enable disc burn support if other mechanisms or preferences disabled it. Needs to be enabled with the `Finder` profile.
- `authenticate`: The system requires authentication.

entries.title: Mobile Accounts
entries.description: The payload that configures mobile accounts on the device.

fields.title: Cachedaccounts CreateAtLogin
fields.description: If `true`, the system creates the mobile account at login time.

fields.title: Cachedaccounts WarnOnCreate
fields.description: If `true`, the system asks the user whether to create the mobile account and it allows the user to not create it.

fields.title: Cachedaccounts WarnOnCreate AllowNever
fields.description: If `true`, the system allows the user to stop the prompts about mobile account creation every time the user logs in. This key is only valid if `com.apple.cachedaccounts.WarnOnCreate` is `true`.

fields.title: Cachedaccounts Expiry Delete DisusedSeconds
fields.description: The minimum number of seconds a mobile account can exist before the system makes an automatic attempt to remove the mobile account. Set to `0` to attempt removing it at the next login or logout. Set to `-1` to never attempt removing the mobile account.

fields.title: Cachedaccounts AskForSecureTokenAuthBypass
fields.description: If `true`, the system bypasses the secure token authorization dialog. This dialog only appears on APFS volumes.

entries.title: Network Proxy Configuration
entries.description: The payload that configures network proxies for a device.

fields.title: Proxies
fields.description: The dictionary containing all the proxies for this device.

entries.title: Network Usage Rules
entries.description: The payload that configures network-usage rules.

fields.title: ApplicationRules
fields.description: An array of application rules, that apply to only managed apps.

fields.title: SIMRules
fields.description: An array of SIM rules, that apply to all apps.

entries.title: Notifications
entries.description: The payload that configures notifications.

notes: Requires supervised devices on at least one platform.

fields.title: Notification Settings
fields.description: An array of notification settings dictionaries.

entries.title: NSExtension Management
entries.description: The payload that configures the extensions that the system allows or disallows to run on the device.

fields.title: AllowedExtensions
fields.description: An array of bundle identifiers for allowed extensions.

fields.title: DeniedExtensions
fields.description: An array of bundle identifiers for extensions that the system doesn't allow to run.

fields.title: DeniedExtensionPoints
fields.description: An array of extension points for extensions that the system doesn't allow to run.

entries.title: Parental Control: Dictation and Profanity
entries.description: The payload that configures parental control for dictation and profanity.

fields.title: Profanity Allowed
fields.description: If `false`, suppresses profanity. Use `forceAssistantProfanityFilter` in Restrictions instead.

fields.title: Ironwood Allowed
fields.description: If `false`, disables dictation. Use `allowDictation` in Restrictions instead.

entries.title: Parental Controls: Application Restrictions
entries.description: The payload that configures parental controls for apps.

fields.title: FamilyControlsEnabled
fields.description: If `true`, enables app access restrictions.

fields.title: WhiteList
fields.description: The allow list of app item dictionaries.

fields.title: PathBlackList
fields.description: The paths to apps in the deny list. This property is deprecated in macOS 10.15 and later.

fields.title: PathWhiteList
fields.description: The paths to apps in the allow list. This property is deprecated in macOS 10.15 and later.

entries.title: Parental Controls: Content Filter
entries.description: The payload that configures the parental control web content filters.

fields.title: RestrictWeb
fields.description: If `true`, enables web content filters.

fields.title: UseContentFilter
fields.description: If `true`, filters content automatically.

fields.title: AllowListEnabled
fields.description: If `true`, enables web content filters.

fields.title: WhitelistEnabled
fields.description: Use `allowListEnabled` instead.

fields.title: SiteAllowList
fields.description:
An array of sites that defines an allow list. If specified, this defines additional allowed sites besides those in the automated allow list and deny list, including disallowed adult sites.

This key is required if `allowListEnabled` is `true`.

fields.title: SiteWhitelist
fields.description: Use `siteAllowList` instead.

fields.title: FilterAllowList
fields.description: The array of URLs that defines an allow list. When `restrictWeb` and `useContentFilter` are enabled, only URLs in the allow list are available to the user.

fields.title: FilterWhitelist
fields.description: Use `filterAllowList` instead.

fields.title: FilterDenyList
fields.description: The array of URLs that defines a deny list. When `restrictWeb` and `useContentFilter` are enabled, no URLs in the deny list are available to the user.

fields.title: FilterBlacklist
fields.description: Use `filterDenyList` instead.

entries.title: Parental Controls: Dashboard Widget Restrictions
entries.description: The payload that configures allowed dashboard widgets.

fields.title: WhiteListEnabled
fields.description: If `true`, enables the widget allow list.

fields.title: WhiteList
fields.description: An array of widget item dictionaries that are allowed.

entries.title: Parental Controls: Dictionary
entries.description: The payload that configures parental control dictionary restrictions.

fields.title: ParentalControl
fields.description: If `true`, enables parental controls dictionary restrictions.

entries.title: Parental Controls: Game Center
entries.description: The payload that configures Game Center parental controls.

fields.title: GKFeatureGameCenterAllowed
fields.description: If `true`, enables Game Center.

fields.title: GKFeatureAccountModificationAllowed
fields.description: If `true`, allows account modifications.

fields.title: GKFeatureAddingGameCenterFriendsAllowed
fields.description: If `true`, allows adding Game Center friends.

fields.title: GKFeatureMultiplayerGamingAllowed
fields.description: If `true`, allows multiplayer gaming.

entries.title: Parental Controls: Time Limits
entries.description: The payload that configures parental control time limits.

fields.title: FamilyControlsEnabled
fields.description: If `true`, enables time limits.

fields.title: Time Limits
fields.description: The time limits to enforce if `familyControlsEnabled` is enabled.

entries.title: Passcode
entries.description: The payload that configures a passcode policy.

fields.title: Allow Simple Value
fields.description: If `false`, the system prevents use of a simple passcode. A simple passcode contains repeated characters, or increasing or decreasing characters, such as `123` or `CBA`.

fields.title: Require Passcode on Device
fields.description: If `true`, the system forces the user to enter a PIN.

fields.title: Maximum Number of Failed Attempts
fields.description:
The number of failed passcode attempts that the system allows the user before it erases or locks the device. After six failed attempts, the device imposes a time delay before the user can enter a passcode again. The time delay increases with each failed attempt. On macOS, set `minutesUntilFailedLoginReset` to define the time delay. The time delay begins after the sixth attempt, so if `MaximumFailedAttempts` is six or lower, the system has no time delay and triggers the erase or lock as soon as the user exceeds the limit.

After the final failed attempt, the system locks a macOS device, or securely erases all data and settings from an iOS, visionOS, or watchOS device.

fields.title: Auto-Lock
fields.description:
The maximum number of minutes for which the device can be idle without the user unlocking it, before the system locks it. When this limit is reached, the system locks the device and the passcode is required to unlock it. The user can edit this setting, but the value can't exceed the `maxInactivity` value.

On macOS, the system translates this inactivity value to screen-saver settings. The maximum value for macOS is `60`.

Setting this key removes the `never` option in the Settings UI on user enrolled devices.

fields.title: Maximum Passcode Age
fields.description: The number of days for which the passcode can remain unchanged. After this number of days, the system forces the user to change the passcode before it unlocks the device.

fields.title: Minimum Number of Complex Characters
fields.description:
The minimum number of complex characters that a passcode needs to contain. A _complex_ character is a character other than a number or a letter, such as `&`, `%`, `$`, and `#`.

The system ignores this property for user enrollments.

fields.title: Minimum Passcode Length
