fields.description: An array of Network Access Identifier Realm names used for Wi-Fi Hotspot 2.0 negotiation.

fields.title: MCC/MNCs
fields.description: An array of Mobile Country Code/Mobile Network Code (MCC/MNC) pairs used for Wi-Fi Hotspot 2.0 negotiation. Each string must contain exactly six digits.

fields.title: Disable Captive Network Detection
fields.description: If `true`, the system bypasses Captive Network detection when the device connects to the network.

fields.title: QoS Marking Policy
fields.description: A dictionary that contains the list of apps that the system allows to benefit from L2 and L3 marking. When this dictionary isn't present, the system allows all apps to use L2 and L3 marking when the Wi-Fi network supports Cisco QoS fast lane.

fields.title: SetupModes
fields.description: An array of strings that contain the type of connection mode to attach.

fields.title: EnableIPv6
fields.description: If `true`, enables IPv6 on this interface.

fields.title: Certificate Required
fields.description: If `true`, allows for two-factor authentication for EAP-TTLS, PEAP, or EAP-FAST. If `false`, allows for zero-factor authentication for EAP-TLS.

fields.title: Proxy Server
fields.description: The proxy server's network address.

fields.title: Proxy Server Port
fields.description: The proxy server's port number.

fields.title: Proxy Username
fields.description: The user name used to authenticate to the proxy server.

fields.title: Proxy Password
fields.description: The password used to authenticate to the proxy server.

fields.title: Proxy PAC URL
fields.description: The URL of the PAC file that defines the proxy configuration.

fields.title: Proxy PAC Fallback Allowed
fields.description: If `true`, allows connecting directly to the destination if the PAC file is unreachable.

fields.title: Disable MAC address randomization during association
fields.description:
If `true,` disables MAC address randomization for a Wi-Fi network while associated with that network. This feature also shows a privacy warning in Settings indicating that the network has reduced privacy protections.

If `false`, then the system enables MAC address randomization on iOS, watchOS, and visionOS.

This value is only locked when MDM installs the profile. If the profile is manually installed, the system sets the value but the user can change it.

fields.title: Allow Join Before First Unlock
fields.description:
If `true`, the device makes this network available for joining before the device is unlocked for the first time following a reboot, on a device configured for return to service. Any network credentials are placed into Class D storage within the keychain, and information about the network is stored on disk in Class D.

There are several restrictions on the use of this flag:

- This property is only available in the return to service mode.
- Only one network can be designated as available before first unlock.
- `EAPClientConfiguration` must not be present.
- If `IsHotspot` is present, it must be set to `false`.
- `QoSMarkingPolicy` must not be present.
- If `ProxyType` is present, it must be set to `None`.

The device fails to install the profile payload if any of these conditions are not met.

entries.title: Wi-Fi Managed Settings
entries.description: The payload that configures managed Wi-Fi settings.

fields.title: RequireAdminForIBSS
fields.description: If `true`, requires administrator authorization to enable IBSS.

fields.title: RequireAdminForAirPortNetworkChange
fields.description: If `true`, requires administrator authorization for network changes.

fields.title: RequireAdminToTurnAirPortOnOff
fields.description: If `true`, requires administrator authorization to turn Wi-Fi on or off.

entries.title: Xsan
entries.description: The payload that configures an Xsan client system.

fields.title: SanName
fields.description: The name of the SAN. This key is required for all Xsan SANs. The name must exactly match the name of the SAN defined in the metadata server.

fields.title: SanConfigURLs
fields.description:
An array of LDAP URLs where Xsan systems can obtain SAN configuration updates. There should be one entry for each Xsan MDC.

This key is required for all Xsan SANs.

Example URL: `ldaps://mdc1.example.com:389`.

fields.title: Fsnameservers
fields.description:
An array of storage area network (SAN) File System Name Server coordinators. The list should contain the same addresses in the same order as the metadata controller (MDC) `/Library/Preferences/Xsan/fsnameservers` file.

This key is required for StorNext SANs.

fields.title: SanAuthMethod
fields.description:
The authentication method for the SAN. This key is required for all Xsan SANs. It's optional for StorNext SANs but should be set if the StorNext SAN uses an `auth_secret` file.



Only one value is accepted: `auth_secret`

fields.title: SharedSecret
fields.description: The shared secret used for Xsan network authentication. This key is required when the `sanAuthMethod` key is present. The value should equal the content of the MDC's `/Library/Preferences/Xsan/.auth_secret` file.

entries.title: Xsan Preferences
entries.description: The payload that configures the Xsan preferences that define the volumes that automatically mount at startup.

fields.title: OnlyMount
fields.description: An array of Xsan or StorNext volume names. The Xsan client attempts to automatically mount these volumes at startup. The system administrator can mount additional volumes manually by using the `xsanctl(8)` mount command.

fields.title: DenyMount
fields.description: An array of Xsan or StorNext volume names. If no `onlyMount` array is present, the Xsan client automatically attempts to mount all SAN volumes except the volumes in this array. The system administrator can mount those volumes manually by using the `xsanctl(8)` mount command.

fields.title: DenyDLC
fields.description: An array of StorNext volume names. If the Xsan client is attempting to mount a volume named in this array, the client only mounts the volume if its logical units (LUNs) are available through Fibre Channel. It doesn't attempt to mount the volume using Distributed LAN Client (DLC).

fields.title: PreferDLC
fields.description: An array of StorNext volume names. If the Xsan client is attempting to mount a volume named in this array, the Xsan client attempts to mount the volume using DLC. If DLC isn't available, the client attempts to mount the volume if its LUNs are available through Fibre Channel. The volume name must not also appear in `denyDLC`.

fields.title: UseDLC
fields.description: If `true`, use the DLC for all volumes.
